section_name: Crossing Sidewalk or Hike and Bike Trail
text:
(a) A person may not drive a motor vehicle on a sidewalk, sidewalk area, or hike and bike trail except on a permanent or authorized temporary driveway.

(b) Subsection (a) does not prohibit the operation of a motor vehicle on a hike and bike trail in connection with maintenance of the trail.

(c) In this section, “hike and bike trail” means a trail designed for the exclusive use of pedestrians, bicyclists, or both.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 545. Operation & Movement of Vehicles
##### § 545.422 — Crossing Sidewalk or Hike and Bike Trail

(a) A person may not drive a motor vehicle on a sidewalk, sidewalk area, or hike and bike trail except on a permanent or authorized temporary driveway.

(b) Subsection (a) does not prohibit the operation of a motor vehicle on a hike and bike trail in connection with maintenance of the trail.

(c) In this section, “hike and bike trail” means a trail designed for the exclusive use of pedestrians, bicyclists, or both.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995. Amended by Acts 1997, 75th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 30.116(a), eff. Sept. 1, 1997.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: A person may not drive a motor vehicle on a sidewalk, sidewalk area, or hike and bike trail except on a permanent or authorized temporary driveway.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: Subsection (a) does not prohibit the operation of a motor vehicle on a hike and bike trail in connection with maintenance of the trail.

subsections.label: c
subsections.text: In this section, “hike and bike trail” means a trail designed for the exclusive use of pedestrians, bicyclists, or both.
